blocks:
Surah As-Sajdah

Meccan, and it is 30 verses

(١) الم (1) Alif Lām Mīm. (٢) تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتابِ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (2) The sending down of the Book, there is no doubt in it, is from the Lord of all the worlds. (٣) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ بَلْ هُوَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ لِتُنْذِرَ قَوْماً ما أَتاهُمْ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ since they were the people of the interval (أهل الفترة). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ ** (that they may be guided) by your warning them. (3) Do they say, 'He has fabricated it'? Rather it is the truth from your Lord, that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, that they may be guided. (٤) اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ Its exegesis has preceded in Surah al-A'raf. ما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا شَفِيعٍ if you transgress His command. أَفَلا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ by the admonitions of Allah. (4) Allah is He who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, then settled on the Throne. You have besides Him no guardian nor any intercessor. Will you not then take admonition?**

(٥) يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ مِنَ السَّماءِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِي يَوْمٍ كانَ مِقْدارُهُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. (5) He directs the command from the heaven to the earth; then it ascends toward Him in a day whose measure is a thousand years by your reckoning. Al-Qummi said: It means the affairs which He directs, and the command (الأمر) and prohibition (النهي) which He commanded, and the deeds of the servants. All of this is manifested on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and the measure of that Day will be a thousand years of the years of the world (الدنيا). And narrations (أخبار) concerning this meaning have already preceded in Surat al-Hajj.

(٦) ذلِكَ عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ ** (6) He is the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen. So He manages its affair (أمرها) according to wisdom, the Almighty (الْعَزِيزُ), the Dominant over His command (الغالب على أمره), the All-merciful (الرَّحِيمُ) towards the servants in His management. (٧) الَّذِي أَحْسَنَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقَهُ ** (7) who perfected everything that He created, providing it fully with what it is prepared for and what is suitable for it, according to wisdom and benefit (المصلحة). And it was read (وقرء) with the opening (فتح) of the lām. وَبَدَأَ خَلْقَ الْإِنْسانِ مِنْ طِينٍ ** and He originated the creation of man from clay. Al-Qummi said: He is Adam. (٨) ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُ ** (8) Then He made his progeny his offspring (ذريته), named thus because it slips away (تنسل) from him, meaning it separates (تنفصل). مِنْ سُلالَةٍ ** from an extract Al-Qummi regarding نَسْلَهُ ** (his progeny) said: meaning his child (ولده). Regarding مِنْ سُلالَةٍ ** (from an extract), he said: It is the essence (الصّفوة) of food and drink. مِنْ ماءٍ مَهِينٍ ** of a despicable fluid, He said: The drop (النطفة), the semen (المني).

(٩) ثُمَّ سَوَّاهُ ** (Then He proportioned him) He straightened him by shaping his limbs as they ought to be. Al-Qummi: His transformation (استحاله) from a drop of semen (نطفة) to a clot (علقة), and from a clot to a lump of flesh (مضغة), until He breathed the Spirit (الرّوح) into him. وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِهِ ** (and breathed into him of His Spirit) The attribution to Himself is for honor (تشريفاً) and to show that he is a wondrous creation (خلق عجيب), and that he possesses a certain status (شأناً) that has some connection (مناسبة ما) to the Divine Presence (الحضرة الربوبيّة). And for this reason, it was said: "Whoever knows his soul has known his Lord." Narrations (أخبار) concerning the meaning of the Spirit (الرّوح) have already passed in Surat al-Hijr. وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ** (and made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts) Specifically so that you may hear, see, and understand (تعقلوا). قَلِيلاً ما تَشْكُرُونَ ** (Little do you thank) A little gratitude.

(١٠) وَقالُوا أَإِذا ضَلَلْنا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (10) And they say, 'When we are lost in the earth, meaning, we become dust mixed with the dust of the earth, such that we are not distinguished from it, or we disappear into it. And it was read by omitting the hamzah (الهمزة). In al-Jawami', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He read it with the undotted letter (المهملة) and the kasrah on the lām (اللّام), derived from (صَلَّ اللَّحْمُ) when the meat rotted (إذا أنتن). أَإِنَّا لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ** shall we be in a new creation?' meaning, our creation will be renewed. And it was read by omitting the hamzah (الهمزة). بَلْ هُمْ بِلِقاءِ رَبِّهِمْ كافِرُونَ. Rather, they are faithless to the encounter with their Lord. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): It means the Resurrection (البعث), and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named it His encounter (لقائه).

(١١)** قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ الَّذِي وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَى رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ ** (11) Say: 'The one who takes you away completely is the Angel of Death, who has been charged with you, then you will be brought back to your Lord.'

** قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ ** takes your souls completely, leaving nothing of them, and leaving none of you remaining مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ الَّذِي وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ** with seizing your spirits and counting your appointed times. ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ** for the reckoning and the recompense.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "When I was taken on the Night Journey (الإسراء) to the heaven, I saw an angel among the angels holding a tablet of light in his hand. He did not turn right or left, focusing intently, appearing like one who is sorrowful. So I asked: 'Who is this, O Gabriel (جبرئيل)?' He replied: 'This is the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), busy seizing souls.' I said: 'Bring me closer to him, O Gabriel (جبرئيل), so I may speak to him.' So he brought me closer to him. I said to him: 'O Angel of Death (ملك الموت), is it true that every person who has died, or who will die later, has his soul seized by you?' He said: 'Yes.' I asked: 'And do you attend to them yourself?' He said: 'Yes. The entire world, in comparison to what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has subjected it to me and enabled me to control it, is nothing but like a dirham in the palm of a man, which he turns over however he wills. And there is no dwelling in the world except that I enter it five times every day. And I say, when the people of the house weep over their dead one: 'Do not weep over him, for I have a return visit to you, and another return visit, until none of you remains.'"

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Death is sufficient as a calamity (طامّة), O Gabriel (جبرئيل)." Gabriel (جبرئيل) replied: "What comes after death is a greater and mightier calamity (أطمّ وأعظم) than death."

(١٢) ** وَلَوْ تَرى إِذِ الْمُجْرِمُونَ ناكِسُوا رُؤُسِهِمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ** (12) Were you to see when the guilty hang their heads before their Lord! out of shame and disgrace, saying, "Our Lord," رَبَّنا أَبْصَرْنا ** (Our Lord! We saw) what You promised us, وَسَمِعْنا ** (and we heard) from You the confirmation of Your messengers, فَارْجِعْنا ** (So send us back) to the world, so that we may act righteously. Indeed, we are certain, since no doubt remains for us regarding what we have witnessed. Al-Qummi (said regarding the verse): أَبْصَرْنا وَسَمِعْنا ** (We saw and we heard) in the world, but we did not act upon it.

(13) ** وَلَوْ شِئْنا لَآتَيْنا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُداها ** (13) Had We wished, We would have certainly given every soul its guidance. [Meaning] that by which it is guided to faith (الإيمان) and righteous deed (العمل الصالح) through granting success (التوفيق) for it. Al-Qummi said: ** وَلَوْ شِئْنا ** (Had We wished) to make all of them infallible (معصومين), We would have been able [to do so]. ** وَلكِنْ حَقَّ الْقَوْلُ مِنِّي ** (But My word has become due:) My decree (قضائي) was established and My threat (وعيدي) preceded: لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ (I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together).

(١٤) فَذُوقُوا بِما نَسِيتُمْ لِقاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هذا إِنَّا نَسِيناكُمْ. (14) So taste [the punishment] because you forgot the encounter of this day of yours; We too have forgotten you. Al-Qummi (said): meaning, We abandoned you (تركناكم). وَذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْخُلْدِ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (And taste the eternal punishment because of what you used to do) which is disbelief (التكذيب) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). (١٥) إِنَّما يُؤْمِنُ بِآياتِنَا الَّذِينَ إِذا ذُكِّرُوا بِها ** (15) Only those believe in Our signs who, when they are reminded of them, (meaning) they are admonished by them, خَرُّوا سُجَّداً ** fall down in prostration, out of fear of Allah's punishment, وَسَبَّحُوا بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ ** and glorify their Lord with praise, and they declare Him free from what is unfitting for Him, such as inability concerning the Resurrection (البعث), praising Him, thanking Him for what He granted them success in regarding Islam and for giving them guidance (الهدى). وَهُمْ لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** and they are not arrogant, about faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة).

(١٦) تَتَجافى جُنُوبُهُمْ ** (16) (Their sides forsake their beds) They rise and move away from the resting places (الْمَضاجِع), which are the mattresses and places of sleep.

In al-Majma, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): They are those who perform the tahajjud prayer at night, who rise from their beds for prayer, يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ (calling their Lord), supplicating to Him خَوْفاً (in fear) of His wrath وَطَمَعاً ** (and hope) in His mercy, وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ ** (and spend out of what We have provided them) in ways of goodness (وجوه الخير).

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "Perhaps you think that the people did not sleep? This body must be rested until its breath (نفسه) departs. When the breath departs, the body rests, and the spirit (الرّوح) returns with strength for action." He said: "It was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) (peace be upon him) and his followers (شيعتنا). They sleep in the first part of the night, but when two-thirds of the night has passed, or as much as Allah wills, they hasten to their Lord, desiring, fearing, and hoping for what is with Him. So Allah mentioned them in His Book and informed you of what He has given them: that He has settled them in His proximity, admitted them into His Paradise, secured them from their fear, and removed their terror."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), and in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Shall I not inform you of the doors of goodness?" It was said: "Yes." He said: "Fasting is a shield (جنّة) from the Fire, and charity (الصدقة) expiates sin, and a man's standing in the depth of the night seeking the Face of Allah." In one narration: "remembering Allah (يذكر الله)." And in another: "communing secretly with his Lord (يناجي ربّه)." Then He recited this verse: تَتَجافى جُنُوبُهُمْ. (Their sides forsake their beds.)

In al-Amali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "They do not sleep until they have prayed al-Atamah (العتمة)."

(١٧) فَلا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ ما أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ ** (No soul knows what has been kept hidden for them) And it was read with the sukun on the yā [of أُخْفِيَ] مِنْ قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ (as a comfort to the eyes) from that which their eyes find comfort in, جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (as a reward for what they used to do).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no good deed that a servant performs except that its reward is mentioned in the Quran, except for the Night Prayer (صلاة الليل). For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not make clear its reward due to its great significance with Him. So, His glorious mention said: تَتَجافى جُنُوبُهُمْ (Their sides keep away) up to His saying **يَعْمَلُونَ ** (they used to do).

Then He said: Indeed, Allah has an honor (كرامة) for His believing servants every Friday. When Friday comes, Allah sends an angel to the believer with two garments (حلّتان). He reaches the gate of Paradise and says: "Ask permission for me to enter upon so-and-so." It is said to him: "This is the messenger of your Lord at the gate." He says to his wives: "What do you see upon me that is most beautiful?" They say: "O our master, by Him Who permitted you Paradise, we have not seen anything upon you more beautiful than this which your Lord has sent to you." So, he wraps one around his waist (يَتَّزِرُ) and drapes the other over his shoulder (يَتَعَطَّفُ). He does not pass by anything except that it illuminates for him until he reaches the appointed place. When they gather, the Lord, blessed and exalted is He, manifests Himself to them. When they look at Him, they fall down prostrate. He says: "My servants, raise your heads. This is not a day of prostration, nor a day of worship. The burden (المؤنة) has been lifted from you." They say: "O Lord, and what is better than what You have given us? You have given us Paradise!" He says: "You shall have seventy times what is in your hands." So, the believer returns every Friday with seventy times what is in his hands. This is His saying: وَلَدَيْنا مَزِيدٌ ** (and with Us is more). And that is the day of Friday. Its night is a glorious night (ليلة غرّاء), and its day is a radiant day (يوم أزهر). So, increase therein the glorification (التسبيح), magnification (التكبير), declaration of oneness (التهليل), praise of Allah, and prayers upon Muhammad and his Household. He said: The believer passes by, and he does not pass by anything except that it illuminates for him until he reaches his wives. They say: "By Him Who permitted us Paradise, O our master, we have never seen you more beautiful than this hour." He says: "Indeed, I have looked upon the Light of my Lord." Then He said: Indeed, his wives neither deceive (لا يغرن), nor menstruate (لا يحضن), nor are they ill-tempered (لا يصلفن). The narrator said: I said: "May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), I wanted to ask you about something I am shy of." He said: "Ask." I said: "Is there singing (غناء) in Paradise?" He said: "Indeed, in Paradise there is a tree whose winds Allah commands, and they blow, striking that tree with sounds whose beauty the creatures have never heard the like of." Then He said: "This is compensation for the one who abandoned listening to singing (السماع للغناء) in the world out of fear of Allah."

He said: I said: "May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), increase me." So, He said: "Indeed, Allah created a Garden (جنّة) with His Hand, which no eye has seen and no creature has been informed of. The Lord opens it every morning and says: 'Increase in fragrance (ريحاً) and increase in goodness (طيباً).' And that is the saying of Allah: فَلا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ ما أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ مِنْ قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (No soul knows what has been kept hidden for them as a comfort to the eyes, as a reward for what they used to do)."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them both): They said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his Household and grant them peace) said: "When I was taken on the Night Journey (أسري بي), I saw in Paradise a river whiter than milk, sweeter than honey, and straighter than an arrow. In it were ewers (أباريق) equal to the number of the stars. On its banks were domes of red ruby and white pearl. Then Gabriel struck it with his wings, and behold, it was fragrant musk (مسكة ذفرة). Then he said: 'By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his Household and grant them peace), indeed in Paradise there is a tree that rustles with glorification (يتصفّق بالتسبيح) with a sound that neither the first nor the last generations have heard. It bears fruit like pomegranates. It casts its fruit to the man, and he splits it open to find seventy garments (حلّة). The believers are upon thrones (الكراسي), and they are the radiant ones (الغرّ المحجّلون) wherever they wish in Paradise. While they are thus, a woman looks down upon them from above, saying: 'Glory be to Allah! O servant of Allah, we have no power (دولة) over you.' He says: 'Who are you?' She says: 'I am one of those concerning whom Allah said: فَلا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ ما أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ مِنْ قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ. (No soul knows what has been kept hidden for them as a comfort to the eyes.)'"

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his Household and grant them peace): Allah the Almighty says: "I have prepared for My righteous servants what no eye has seen, nor ear has heard, nor has it occurred to the heart of man, let alone what I have informed you of. Recite, if you wish: فَلا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ ** (No soul knows) the Ayah."

I say: بله (pronounced like ككتف) means "leave" (دع) or "besides" (سوى).

(18) أَفَمَنْ كانَ مُؤْمِناً كَمَنْ كانَ فاسِقاً ** (Is he who is a faithful man like him who is a transgressor?) one who has departed from faith لا يَسْتَوُونَ ** (They are not equal.) in honor and reward. (19) أَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَلَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْمَأْوى نُزُلاً ** (As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, for them are the Gardens of the Abode, as a hospitality) al-Nuzul (النزل) is what is prepared for the guest (النازل) of food, drink, and gift (صلة) بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (for what they used to do.) (20) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا فَمَأْواهُمُ النَّارُ كُلَّما أَرادُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْها أُعِيدُوا فِيها ** (But as for those who transgressed, their abode is the Fire. Whenever they desire to leave it, they will be sent back into it,) an expression denoting their eternity therein **وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ ذُوقُوا عَذابَ النَّارِ الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ ** (and they will be told, 'Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.') as an humiliation for them and an increase in their rage.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Indeed, when they enter Hell (جهنّم), they will fall into it for a distance of seventy years' journey. When they reach its bottom, Hell (جهنّم) will sigh/breathe heavily (زفرت بهم), and when they reach its top, they will be struck down with iron maces (مقامع الحديد). This is their condition.

**(٢١) وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُمْ مِنَ الْعَذابِ الْأَدْنى دُونَ الْعَذابِ الْأَكْبَرِ ** (21) And We will surely make them taste of the lesser punishment, before the greater punishment, meaning, before they reach the Hereafter.

Al-Qummi said: الْعَذابِ الْأَدْنى ** (the lesser punishment) is the punishment of the Return (الرجعة) by the sword. لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ** (that they may return). He said: For they will return in the Return (الرجعة) until they are punished.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that الْعَذابِ الْأَدْنى ** (the lesser punishment) is the punishment of the grave. He said: And the majority of the narrations from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both) state: that the lesser punishment (الْعَذابِ الْأَدْنى) is the Beast (الدابّة) and the Dajjal (الدجال).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said: Indeed, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and al-Walid ibn 'Uqbah disputed. The wicked one (الفاسق), al-Walid ibn 'Uqbah, said: "By Allah, I am more eloquent than you in speech, sharper than you in status (شأناً), and better at kneeling (جثواً) in the squadron (الكتيبة)." So Ali (peace be upon him) said: "Be silent! You are merely a wicked one (فاسق)." Then Allah sent down these verses.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him) in a narration of his: "As for you, O Walid ibn 'Uqbah, by Allah, I do not blame you for hating Ali, for he lashed you eighty stripes for drinking wine, and he killed your father by execution (صبراً) with His own hand on the Day of Badr. How can you revile him when Allah has named him a believer (مؤمناً) in ten verses of the Quran, and named you a wicked one (فاسقاً)? And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: أَفَمَنْ كانَ مُؤْمِناً كَمَنْ كانَ فاسِقاً لا يَسْتَوُونَ. (Is he who is a believer like him who is a wicked one? They are not equal.)

I say: The narrations are widespread (مستفيضة) through the paths of the general populace (العامّة) and the select (الخاصّة) that these verses were revealed concerning Ali (peace be upon him) and al-Walid.

(٢٢) ** وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ ذُكِّرَ بِآياتِ رَبِّهِ ثُمَّ أَعْرَضَ عَنْها ** (22) Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, then turns away from them? and did not reflect upon them. And ثُمَّ (then) is used to indicate the remoteness (الاستبعاد) of turning away from them, despite their extreme clarity and their guidance to the causes of happiness, after being reminded of them. ** إِنَّا مِنَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ مُنْتَقِمُونَ ** (22) Indeed, We will take vengeance upon the guilty. So how (will it be) concerning one who was a greater wrongdoer than every wrongdoer?

(٢٣) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ فَلا تَكُنْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْ لِقائِهِ ** (And certainly We gave Moses the Book, so do not be in doubt about meeting Him) It was said: concerning the meeting of Moses (peace be upon him) with his Lord in the Hereafter. Likewise, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. وَجَعَلْناهُ هُدىً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ.** (and We made it a guidance for the Children of Israel.)

(24) وَجَعَلْنا مِنْهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنا لَمَّا صَبَرُوا (And We appointed from among them imams guiding by Our command, when they were patient.) And it was recited with the kasrah of the lām and takhfif (lightness).

Al-Qummi said: It was in the knowledge of Allah that they would be patient regarding what afflicted them, so He appointed them as imams (أَئِمَّةً).

And narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), he said: The imams in the Book of Allah are two types of imams. The Almighty Allah said: وَجَعَلْنا مِنْهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنا (And We appointed from among them imams guiding by Our command,) not by the command of the people. They put the command of Allah before their own command and the judgment of Allah before their own judgment—the hadith [continues]. وَكانُوا بِآياتِنا يُوقِنُونَ (and they used to have certainty of Our signs.) due to their deep scrutiny of the observation into them.

(٢٥) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (25) Your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection He judges, thereby distinguishing the truth from the falsehood by distinguishing the one who is truthful (المحقّ) from the one who is false (المبطل) فِيما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** (in what they used to differ about) concerning the matter of religion (الدين). (٢٦) أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ ** (26) Has it not guided them how many generations We destroyed before them? meaning, the multitude of those whom We destroyed. يَمْشُونَ فِي مَساكِنِهِمْ ** (They walk in their dwellings) It was said that this means the people of Mecca (أهل مكّة) pass by their dwellings during their trade journeys. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ أَفَلا يَسْمَعُونَ ** (There are indeed signs in that. Will they not listen?) a hearing of reflection (تدبّر) and admonition (اتّعاظ).

(27) ** أَوَ لَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا نَسُوقُ الْماءَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ الْجُرُزِ ** (27) Have they not regarded that We drive the water to the barren land which has had its vegetation cut (جُرز)—that is, severed and removed. Al-Qummi said: The desolate land (الأرض الخراب). فَنُخْرِجُ بِهِ زَرْعاً تَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ أَنْعامُهُمْ ** and bring forth thereby crops whereof their cattle eat such as straw and leaves, and their souls (أَنْفُسُهُمْ) (eat) such as grain and fruit. أَفَلا يُبْصِرُونَ ** Will they not then perceive? so that they may infer from it the perfection of His power and His favor.

(٢٨) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْفَتْحُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (28) And they say, 'When will this victory come, if you are truthful?' regarding the promise of it. (٢٩) قُلْ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ لا يَنْفَعُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِيمانُهُمْ وَلا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ ** (29) Say, 'On the day of the victory, the faith of the faithless will not benefit them, nor will they be granted any respite.' nor will they be granted respite (ولا يمهلون). (٣٠) فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَانْتَظِرْ إِنَّهُمْ مُنْتَظِرُونَ (30) So disregard them, and wait; they too are waiting.

Al-Qummi said: This is a parable (مثل) set forth by Allah concerning the Return (الرجعة) and al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله informed them of the news of the Return (الرجعة), they said: مَتى هذَا الْفَتْحُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (When will this victory come, if you are truthful?). And this is connected (معطوفة) to His saying: وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُمْ مِنَ الْعَذابِ الْأَدْنى دُونَ الْعَذابِ الْأَكْبَرِ.** (And We will surely make them taste of the lesser punishment, short of the greater punishment.)

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat al-Sajdah every Friday night, Allah will give him his book (كتابه) in his right hand, and He will not hold him accountable for what he did, and he will be among the companions of Muhammad and his Household (peace be upon him and his Household).

And in Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever longs for Paradise (الجنّة) and its description, let him recite al-Waqi'ah. And whoever loves to look at the description of the Fire (النار), let him recite سجدة لقمان.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The obligatory prostrations (العزائم) are four: اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ (Recite in the Name of your Lord Who created), وَالنَّجْمِ (and al-Najm), وَتَنْزِيلُ السَّجْدِ (and Tanzil al-Sajd), and حم السّجدة (Ha Mim al-Sajdah). And Allah knows best (والله يعلم).
